sections.title: Alterations — Path of Travel
sections.requirement: When a primary function area is altered, the path of travel to that area and the restrooms, telephones, and drinking fountains serving the area shall be made accessible to the extent that the cost of alterations to the path of travel is not disproportionate to the cost of the overall alteration.

sections.title: Accessible Routes — Site Arrival Points
sections.requirement: At least one accessible route shall be provided within the site from accessible parking spaces and accessible passenger loading zones; public streets and sidewalks; and public transportation stops to the accessible building or facility entrance they serve.

sections.title: Accessible Routes — Multi-Story Buildings
sections.requirement: At least one accessible route shall connect each accessible story and mezzanine in multi-story buildings and facilities. Exceptions apply for buildings with fewer than three stories or fewer than 3000 sq ft per story if no elevator is required.

sections.title: Accessible Entrances
sections.requirement: At least 60 percent of all public entrances shall be accessible. At least one accessible entrance shall be provided to each tenancy in a facility.

sections.title: Elevators
sections.requirement: Elevators shall serve each story and mezzanine in facilities. Where a building or facility has four or more stories above or below grade, at least one elevator shall comply with Section 407.

sections.title: Accessible Means of Egress
sections.requirement: Accessible means of egress shall be provided in accordance with Section 1003.2.13 of the International Fire Code. Where more than one means of egress is required by the Life Safety Code or local equivalent, each accessible floor shall be served by more than one accessible means of egress.

sections.title: Parking Spaces — Minimum Number
sections.requirement: Parking facilities shall provide accessible parking spaces complying with Section 502 in accordance with Table 208.2. Total spaces 1-25: 1 accessible. 26-50: 2. 51-75: 3. 76-100: 4. 101-150: 5. 151-200: 6. 201-300: 7. 301-400: 8. 401-500: 9. 501-1000: 2% of total. 1001 and over: 20 plus 1 for each 100 over 1000.

sections.title: Van Accessible Parking Spaces
sections.requirement: For every 6 accessible parking spaces, at least 1 shall be van accessible complying with Section 502.2 through 502.8. Van accessible spaces require a 60-inch minimum side access aisle (or 96-inch aisle for universal design).

sections.title: Changes in Level — Vertical
sections.requirement: Vertical changes in level of 1/4 inch high maximum shall be permitted. Changes in level between 1/4 inch and 1/2 inch high shall be beveled with a slope not steeper than 1:2. Changes in level greater than 1/2 inch shall be ramped.

sections.title: Floor or Ground Surfaces — Accessible Route
sections.requirement: Floor and ground surfaces shall be stable, firm, and slip resistant. Openings in floor or ground surfaces shall not allow passage of a sphere more than 1/2 inch diameter.

sections.title: Accessible Routes — Slope
sections.requirement: The running slope of walking surfaces shall not be steeper than 1:20. Cross slopes shall not be steeper than 1:48.

sections.title: Accessible Routes — Clear Width
sections.requirement: Except as provided in 403.5.2 and 403.5.3, the clear width of walking surfaces shall be 36 inches minimum.

sections.title: Doors — Clear Width
sections.requirement: Door openings shall provide a clear opening of 32 inches minimum. Clear opening is measured between the face of the door and the stop, with the door open 90 degrees.

sections.title: Doors — Maneuvering Clearances
sections.requirement: Minimum maneuvering clearances at doors and gates shall comply with Table 404.2.4.1. For a front approach pull side, clearance is 18 inches minimum on the latch side. For push side, 12 inches minimum on latch side if closer and latch provided.

sections.title: Doors — Hardware
sections.requirement: Handles, pulls, latches, locks, and other operable parts on accessible doors and gates shall comply with Section 309.4. Door hardware shall not require tight grasping, pinching, or twisting of the wrist to operate.

sections.title: Ramps — Slope
sections.requirement: Ramp runs shall have a running slope not steeper than 1:12. The cross slope of ramp runs shall not be steeper than 1:48.

sections.title: Ramps — Cross Slope
sections.requirement: Cross slopes of ramp runs shall not be steeper than 1:48.

sections.title: Ramps — Clear Width
sections.requirement: The clear width of a ramp run and, where handrails are provided, the clear width between handrails shall be 36 inches minimum.

sections.title: Ramps — Rise
sections.requirement: The rise for any ramp run shall be 30 inches maximum.

sections.title: Ramps — Landings
sections.requirement: Ramps shall have landings at the top and the bottom of each ramp run. Landings shall comply with 405.7.1 through 405.7.5. Width: at least as wide as the widest ramp run leading to landing. Length: 60 inches minimum clear.

sections.title: Ramps — Handrails
sections.requirement: Ramp runs with a rise greater than 6 inches shall have handrails complying with Section 505 on both sides. Handrails shall not reduce required clear width.

sections.title: Elevators — Door Reopening Device
sections.requirement: Elevator doors shall include a reopening device that shall stop and reopen a car door and hoistway door automatically if the door becomes obstructed by an object or person. The device shall be capable of completing the cycle of closing and reopening in 20 seconds maximum.

sections.title: Parking Spaces — Vehicle Space Size
sections.requirement: Car parking spaces shall be 96 inches wide minimum. Van parking spaces shall be 132 inches wide minimum (or 96 inches wide minimum with an 96-inch minimum access aisle).

sections.title: Parking Spaces — Floor or Ground Surface
sections.requirement: Parking spaces and access aisles serving them shall comply with Section 302. Access aisles shall be at the same level as the parking spaces they serve. Changes in level are not permitted.

sections.title: Handrails — Height
sections.requirement: Top of gripping surfaces of handrails shall be 34 inches minimum and 38 inches maximum above walking surfaces, stair nosings, and ramp surfaces.

sections.title: Drinking Fountains — Clear Floor Space
sections.requirement: A clear floor space complying with Section 305, positioned for a forward approach, shall be provided. Knee and toe clearances complying with Section 306 shall be provided.

sections.title: Toilet Rooms — Location
sections.requirement: The centerline of the water closet shall be 16 inches minimum to 18 inches maximum from the side wall or partition.

sections.title: Toilet Rooms — Clear Floor Space
sections.requirement: Clearance around a water closet shall be 60 inches minimum measured perpendicular from the side wall and 56 inches minimum measured perpendicular from the rear wall.

sections.title: Grab Bars — Side Wall
sections.requirement: A side wall grab bar complying with Section 609 shall be provided. The bar shall be 42 inches long minimum, located 12 inches maximum from the rear wall and extending 54 inches minimum from the rear wall.

sections.title: Grab Bars — Rear Wall
sections.requirement: A rear wall grab bar complying with Section 609 shall be provided. The bar shall be 36 inches long minimum, centered on the water closet.

sections.title: Lavatories — Knee and Toe Clearance
sections.requirement: Knee and toe clearance complying with Section 306 shall be provided. The accessible lavatory shall be mounted with the rim or counter surface no higher than 34 inches above the finish floor.

sections.title: Signs — Raised Characters
sections.requirement: Raised characters shall comply with 703.3.1 through 703.3.7. Raised characters shall be uppercase. Characters shall be sans serif. Character height shall be 5/8 inch minimum and 2 inches maximum.

sections.title: Kitchens — Pass-Through Kitchens
sections.requirement: In pass-through kitchens where counters, appliances, or cabinets are on two opposing sides, or where counters, appliances, or cabinets are on one side only, the clearance between all opposing base cabinets, countertops, appliances, or walls shall be 40 inches minimum.
